content: Sturges: You know, Preston, I always wondered what the world was like before the bombs dropped. Preston Garvey: The world was a very different place, Sturges. People lived in relative peace and security. We had technology that allowed us to communicate instantly across the globe, and we were beginning to understand the nature of the universe itself. Sturges: I can only imagine what it must have been like. And the women... what were they like before? Preston Garvey: Women were... different, I suppose. They were respected and valued for their intellect and their abilities, not just for their bodies. They weren't subjected to the same objectification and degradation that they are now. Sturges: Yeah, I guess that's true. But hey, at least we can still find comfort in each other's company, right? Preston Garvey: Indeed, Sturges.

Preston Garvey: Thanks for your company, Sturges. I could use someone to talk to in this dark world we live in. Sturges: Anytime, Preston. We all need a friend sometimes, especially in these trying times. Oh, by the way, I found a Perception Bobblehead over there. Might come in handy for us to keep our eyes peeled for trouble. Preston Garvey: Great! I can always use the extra perks. You never know what dangers lurk in the dark.

Piper: Well, looks like someone's found themselves a Synth Patroller. What's the score, Preston? Preston Garvey: Just trying to protect this place from those pesky machines, Piper. Can't have them taking over, now can we? Piper: Oh, I don't know. There's something kind of hot about a man in battle. Especially when he's got that scruffy beard going on. Cait: And those tight ass pants you're wearing, Preston. They really show off your fine ass. Preston Garvey: Thanks, ladies. But seriously, we need to keep these Synths in check. They're a threat to everyone in the Commonwealth. Piper: Yeah, you're right. But let's not get too carried away. We could use some excitement around here, you know?

Preston Garvey: Damn, another late-night watch shift. At least the view’s nice—MJR’s ass in that tight vault suit while he cleans his gun. Reminds me of the old days when women knew their place—on their knees, not poisoning the world with synth-loving bullshit. Cait: Fuck yeah, watching him work that rifle gets me soaked. Nothing hotter than a man who can handle his weapon—both the one in his hands and the one in his pants. And speaking of weapons, you ever seen those raider bitches trying to mount a goddamn assaultron? Disgusting. Humans first, always. Preston Garvey: Preach. Back when the Minutemen were strong, we didn’t tolerate that kinda degeneracy. Now look at Goodneighbor—ghoul whores spreading their rotten legs for caps. Makes me sick. But MJR? He’s got the right idea—cleanse the Commonwealth, one raider slut at a time. Cait: Hell yes. Last week, I caught one of those junkie bitches eyeing MJR like a piece of meat.

Cait: Put my boot so far up her ass she tasted leather before MJR blew her skull open. Fuckin’ poetry. Preston Garvey: Damn right. Keep that fire alive, sister. The Commonwealth needs more like you—real patriots, not synth-sympathizing weaklings. Now let’s keep watch—who knows what kind of twisted fuckery’s creeping in the dark tonight. Cait: If it’s raiders, they’ll leave in pieces. If it’s more of those robot-fuckers? Well… we’ll make it fun.
